file_insights.insight: New focused module with single responsibility: spawning `bop new` subprocess. Uses anyhow for consistent error propagation. Synchronous Command execution with stderr capture for error messages. Minimal test placeholder acknowledges PATH resolution uncertainty in test environments.
App struct extended minimally with single new field: `newcard_input: String`. Initialized in constructor. Clean separation—no creation logic, just state management for input buffer.

NewCard mode handler fully wired with 4 key cases: Esc (cancel), Enter (create), Backspace (delete), Char (append). Mode switching properly clears input on entry and restore to Normal on exit. Calls `build_columns()` for refresh after creation—best-effort, errors logged non-fatally.
Footer rendering now shows live input buffer in NewCard legend: `new_card_legend(&app.newcard_input)`. Previously hardcoded empty string; now reflects typed ID in real-time.
Module export added for newcard; follows existing pattern (action, detail, etc.). Public re-export of `create_card` function.

patterns_discovered.detail: 'n' clears input buffer, enters NewCard mode; Esc/Enter clears and returns to Normal. Guards against stale input across mode transitions.
Errors from `bop new` are logged to stderr but do NOT crash TUI. Cards refresh happens unconditionally, allowing user to see failure and retry.
After card creation, `build_columns()` called directly to reload filesystem state. Rebuilds entire column structure from `.cards/` root.
Only checks `!id.is_empty()` before calling `bop new`. Format validation delegated to subprocess (binary is authority).
Printable chars pushed to buffer; Backspace pops. Standard terminal input pattern.
Test function includes comment: 'We can't assert success because bop may not be on PATH in test environments.' Recognizes environment variability.

gotchas_discovered.detail: If `bop new` is slow (large template clone, disk I/O), TUI freezes until completion. No spinner/progress feedback to user. May require async refactor for responsive UX at scale.
Code assumes `bop` is on PATH. In self-contained macOS .app or single-binary deployments, may need to resolve bop binary location dynamically (e.g., via current_exe or bundle path).

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No input format validation in widget
gotchas_discovered.detail: Valid card IDs may have naming rules (e.g., team prefix, alphanumeric-only) that aren't checked in TUI. Errors surface only after subprocess runs. Early validation would improve UX.
Widget doesn't check if card ID already exists in `.cards/`. Subprocess will fail, error logged, but no user feedback that ID was already taken.
If card file appears slowly (async move from template, COW clone overhead), refresh may read stale state. No polling/watch mechanism to ensure consistency.

discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS — Inline card creation fully integrated into TUI. NewCard mode enters on 'n', input accumulates, Enter spawns `bop new default <id>`, Esc cancels. Footer shows live input. Refresh fires automatically. Error handling is defensive (logged, non-fatal). Code is minimal, focused, and follows existing patterns (mode switching, subprocess execution, state management).

recommendations.recommendation: Migrate Command execution to async with timeout
recommendations.rationale: Prevent TUI freeze during slow card creation. Spawn in background task, show spinner in footer, update on completion/error. Use tokio::task::spawn_blocking + timeout crate.

recommendations.recommendation: Add input validation for card ID format
recommendations.rationale: Check naming rules (team prefix, alphanumeric, length limits) before subprocess. Fail fast with user feedback in footer ('Invalid ID format: ...').

recommendations.recommendation: Resolve bop binary path relative to current_exe
recommendations.rationale: Ensure packaged app (.app bundle, single binary) can locate bop without PATH. Use std::env::current_exe() and walk up to find cargo workspace root or sibling binary.
Scan `.cards/*/` for existing ID before creation attempt. Show 'Card already exists' warning in footer before subprocess execution.

recommendations.recommendation: Implement polling watch for card filesystem
recommendations.rationale: After creation, wait for card file to appear (up to 1s timeout) before refresh. Ensures build_columns() sees the new card consistently.
